subsection.heading: Establishment.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary shall establish in the Department an Office of Survivors Assistance (in this section referred to as the “Office”) to serve as a resource regarding all benefits and services furnished by the Department—

paragraph.content: to survivors and dependents of deceased veterans; and
to survivors and dependents of deceased members of the Armed Forces.

subsection.heading: Advisory Duties.—
subsection.content: The Office shall serve as a primary advisor to the Secretary on all matters related to the policies, programs, legislative issues, and other initiatives affecting the survivors and dependents described in subsection (a).

subsection.heading: Guidance From Stakeholders.—
subsection.content: In establishing the Office, the Secretary shall seek guidance from interested stakeholders.

subsection.heading: Resources.—
subsection.content: The Secretary shall ensure that appropriate personnel, funding, and other resources are provided to the Office to carry out its responsibilities.

subsection.heading: Inclusion of Information on Office in Annual Report on Department Activities.—
subsection.content: The Secretary shall include in each annual Performance and Accountability report submitted by the Secretary to Congress a description of the activities of the Office during the fiscal year covered by such report.
